mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that lowers discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 70%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in unemployment claims (PAYEMS) indicates tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings expectations, driving S&P 500 gains over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS falls by >50k over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in consumer spending (CPIAUCSL) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions that elevate equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative when both CPIAUCSL and HOUST rise by >1.5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding capacity utilization that lowers corporate cost pressures, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when INDPRO rises by >1.2% over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and stagnant monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) creates real rate compression that drives equity market rallies over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235 and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 6.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off behavior, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE increases by >0.3pp over 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in consumer confidence (RSAFS) combined with weak housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening demand that depresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when both RSAFS and HOUST fall by >2% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects weakening supply chain capacity that raises corporate earnings risks, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 50%+ of periods when INDPRO declines by >1.5% over 1 month.
